steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content:
The sentence uses the correlative conjunction not only … but also to join two parallel prepositional phrases: “not only by cyclones” and “but also by vapor streams.” No punctuation should separate the two parts of a correlative conjunction when they connect parallel phrases.
  • Choice A — 'caused not only by cyclones—': The em dash after “cyclones” creates a false break, making the sentence appear to introduce a list or interruption rather than a parallel structure. ✗
  • Choice B — 'caused—not only by cyclones—': Wrapping “not only by cyclones” in dashes turns it into a parenthetical aside, destroying the parallel “not only … but also” construction. ✗
  • Choice C — 'caused, not only by cyclones,': The commas around “not only by cyclones” also treat it as a parenthetical, incorrectly isolating it from the “but also” clause. ✗
  • Choice D — 'caused not only by cyclones': No punctuation between “not only by cyclones” and the following “but also by vapor streams” — exactly what parallel correlative structure requires. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content: The correlative conjunction “not only … but also” linking two parallel phrases takes no internal punctuation. That matches choice D — D is correct.
